steps.title: Tangent ⊥ radius — find the radius slope
steps.content: A tangent line is always perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency. The radius goes from center $(2, 9)$ to point $(a, -4)$, giving slope: $$m_{\text{radius}} = \frac{-4 - 9}{a - 2} = \frac{-13}{a - 2}.$$

steps.title: Use the negative-reciprocal rule and solve
steps.content: For perpendicular lines, $m_{\text{radius}} = -\frac{1}{m_{\text{tangent}}} = -\frac{5}{6}$. Set up: $$\frac{-13}{a - 2} = -\frac{5}{6} \implies 5(a - 2) = 78 \implies a = \frac{88}{5}.$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.
